spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Interactive surface exists but is limited — more controls or a richer live readout would help.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Page handles several edge cases (errors, fallbacks, feature detection, denials) — not just the happy path.

references.rationale: Cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link as required.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for xml-parsing-in-rust-for-non-xslt-scenarios
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: XML parsing in Rust for non-XSLT scenarios. Chrome replaces libxml-based XML parsing with a Rust implementation for non-XSLT XML (XHR responseXML, DOMParser, fetch xml MIME). Behavioural deltas: stricter well-formedness, parameter-entity handling, DTD limits. No new web-facing API. Authors should not rely on specific libxml error messages.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Add more controls / a richer live readout so the user can drive the feature through its full input space, not just a single canned example.

summary: Workable concept with a couple of gaps. 2 open question(s) queued.
